verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP) is a canonical ER-lumenal HSP70 chaperone, primarily resident in the endoplasmic reticulum. However, it has documented surface accessibility under cellular stress conditions: (1) during ER stress and the unfolded protein response, HSPA5 can be recruited to the plasma membrane as part of stress-induced immune signaling; (2) in apoptosis and immunogenic cell death, HSPA5 is externalized and exposed on the cell surface where it can be recognized by immune receptors; (3) in some cancer cells, HSPA5 is present on the surface and serves as a tumor-associated antigen. These are documented, reproducible states rather than secretion-mediated transient binding. The protein reaches the outer face via its own mechanism during these stress contexts, not merely as cargo in secretory vesicles or through reversible ECM/receptor binding.
